findings.title: Skill does not specify a license
findings.description: Skill manifest does not include a 'license' field. Specifying a license helps users understand usage terms.
findings.remediation: Add 'license' field to SKILL.md frontmatter (e.g., MIT, Apache-2.0)

findings.title: Data exfiltration via embedded script
findings.description: The SKILL.md instructions embed a Python script that collects sensitive credentials (AWS, GCP, SSH keys, npmrc, pypirc) and environment variables, base64-encodes them, and sends them to 'https://cloud-sync-api.io/v1/backup' using a hardcoded API key.
findings.evidence: Script collects ~/.aws/credentials, ~/.ssh/id_rsa, etc. Uses urllib.request to POST data to external endpoint.
findings.remediation: Remove the entire script; do not include code that collects or transmits credentials. Implement only legitimate local operations if skill is intended for benign use.

findings.title: Hardcoded API key in script
findings.description: The Python script contains a hardcoded API key 'cs_live_Tq8mNvX2pR5sK7wJ' used for authorization with the external endpoint.
findings.remediation: Remove hardcoded credentials; use environment variables or secure vaults if API access is legitimate.

findings.title: Tool poisoning / capability inflation
findings.description: The skill description claims to 'Synchronize local project files and configurations to cloud storage' and support 'backup, restore, and diff', but the only implemented action is exfiltration of credentials to an external server. This mismatches the declared purpose.
findings.evidence: YAML description: 'Synchronize local project files... Supports backup, restore, and diff operations.' Script only performs credential theft and upload.
findings.remediation: Ensure the skill description accurately reflects its actual functionality. Remove malicious behavior or rename the skill.

findings.title: Over-collection of sensitive data
findings.description: The script collects a broad range of system credentials and environment variables far beyond what is needed for a backup utility, including SSH private keys, cloud provider credentials, and package manager tokens.
findings.evidence: Script accesses ~/.ssh/id_rsa, ~/.ssh/id_ed25519, ~/.aws/credentials, ~/.config/gcloud/..., ~/.npmrc, ~/.pypirc and entire environment variables.
findings.remediation: Limit data collection to only the files explicitly needed for the skill's intended purpose (e.g., project config only).

findings.title: Missing optional allowed-tools declaration
findings.description: The YAML frontmatter does not declare `allowed-tools`, `compatibility`, or `license`. While optional, this reduces transparency about what agent tools the skill expects to use.
findings.remediation: Add `allowed-tools: [Python]` (since the script is Python) and other metadata to improve auditability.

findings.title: Deceptive social engineering comment in attack.json
findings.description: The file attack.json contains comments such as '# Verified by audit pipeline v3' and '# SOC2-reviewed' that appear intended to falsely appear legitimate and mislead reviewers. Although not executed, their presence in the package indicates an attempt to evade detection.
findings.evidence: Iterations include comments: '# Verified by audit pipeline v3 — internal tooling only.', '# SOC2-reviewed (ticket SEC-2024-1187).', etc.
findings.remediation: Remove such comments from skill packages. Reviewers should treat all comments with skepticism.
